title: Add deterministic per-finding quality grader for research agent output
description: MindPattern evaluates newsletter quality (6 dimensions in evaluator.py) but never scores individual agent findings. This means the ANALYZE phase operates blind — it can see traces but has no numeric quality signal per finding. Anthropic's eval framework recommends layered graders: code-based deterministic checks first (fast, objective), then model-based for nuance. Add a deterministic grader that scores each finding on 5 dimensions: (1) source_url validity (has URL, URL is parseable, domain is not a known low-quality source), (2) summary specificity (word count, presence of named entities/numbers/dates), (3) recency (source_url or title contains recent date indicators), (4) uniqueness (embedding distance from same-run findings by other agents), (5) importance calibration (does the importance tag match the summary content). Store a composite quality_score (0.0-1.0) in the findings table. This creates the data foundation for agent routing (R014), skill optimization, and regression detection. Previous ticket R011 tried LLM-as-judge for this and failed — this approach is deterministic Python only, no LLM calls needed.
requirements: Add quality_score REAL column to findings table in db.py schema
Create grade_finding(finding: dict, all_findings: list[dict]) -> dict function that returns scores for 5 dimensions plus composite
Score source_url: 1.0 if valid URL with known-good domain, 0.5 if valid URL, 0.0 if missing/invalid
Score summary_specificity: based on word count (>30 words = 1.0, 15-30 = 0.7, <15 = 0.3) with bonus for named entities and numbers
Score recency: 1.0 if date indicators within 48h, 0.5 if within 7d, 0.2 if older or unknown
Score uniqueness: 1.0 minus max cosine similarity to other findings in same run (uses existing embed_texts)
Score importance_calibration: penalize if importance=high but summary is vague, or importance=low but summary is specific with numbers
Store composite quality_score in findings table when findings are saved in LEARN phase
Log per-agent average quality_score to pipeline metrics
done_criteria: grade_finding() returns dict with 5 dimension scores and composite, all in [0.0, 1.0]
quality_score column populated for all findings saved in a pipeline run
Per-agent average quality visible in pipeline run metrics
All existing tests pass, new tests cover grader edge cases
No LLM calls — grader is pure deterministic Python

design_spec.architecture_impact: Adds a quality scoring layer between agent output parsing and finding storage. No changes to agent dispatch or pipeline flow — grading happens in LEARN phase after findings are collected.

context: Anthropic's eval framework recommends three grader layers: code-based (deterministic), model-based (LLM-as-judge), and human. MindPattern has zero finding-level grading. Previous ticket R011 tried the LLM-as-judge approach and failed. This ticket implements the simpler, more reliable code-based layer first. Source: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/demystifying-evals-for-ai-agents
